channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This viral Tim Walz content was created by Russia, U.S. intelligence confirms
item.description: Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: US banks brace for open banking era of consumer data sharing
item.description: The measure effectively breaks banks’ hold on crucial data from savings patterns and checking account data to rent payment history — a set up where some firms made switching harder to help boost profits.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity
item.description: AI is one of the most potent tools supplementing the cybersecurity efforts of federal agencies, but DHS is ensuring it's implemented responsibly and securely. The post DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta Suspends Accounts Tracking Celebrities' Private Jets
item.description: Meta suspends accounts tracking private jets of celebrities, citing privacy policy violation
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Developers embracing API-first development, survey says
item.description: Developers are shifting from code-first to API-first thinking when it comes to software development, according to the newly released 2024 State of the API Report from API platform provider Postman.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service
item.description: The Social Security Administration is making IT investments that will make its employees more productive and its services more accessible to its customers. The post SSA gets $30M from TMF for IT upgrades key to better customer service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work has begun on JDK 24, with six features so far proposed for the release: removal of the non-generational mode in the Z Garbage Collector, stream gatherers, a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Wall Street Journal, New York Post sue AI startup
item.description: Media conglomerate News Corp, which owns The Wall Street Journal, New York Post and a number of other media properties, is suing artificial intelligence (AI) startup Perplexity over what it alleges is copyright infringement.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: cPacket doubles the speed of its packet capture appliance
item.description: cPacket Networks this week launched a new appliance designed to capture network traffic and enable network operations teams to achieve better efficiency by monitoring network reliability in real time while also looking backward to investigate prior incidents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Two-thirds of leaders feel genAI will reshape entry-level jobs
item.description: Generative AI is on track to transform entry-level roles across industries, with 64% of leaders expecting these positions to evolve from creation to review and refinement of outputs within the next three years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation
